_note: Conduit fill calculation procedure using Chapter 9 Table 1 (% allowed) + Table 4 (raceway dimensions) + Table 5 (conductor dimensions). Inline tables here are reference; full data lives in chapter9-tables.json.

calculation_procedure.action: Identify the conductor types and counts to be installed
calculation_procedure.data_needed: Wire type (THHN, THWN-2, XHHW-2, etc.) + AWG/kcmil size + quantity

calculation_procedure.action: Look up each conductor's external area
calculation_procedure.data_needed: Chapter 9 Table 5 — gives external area in² per conductor by insulation type and size
calculation_procedure._note: External area, NOT copper cross-section (kcmil) — external area includes the insulation

calculation_procedure.action: Determine allowed conduit fill percentage from Table 1
calculation_procedure._note: ≥3 conductors = 40% is the most common case in commercial work

calculation_procedure.action: Look up raceway internal area from Table 4
calculation_procedure.data_needed: Raceway type (RMC, IMC, EMT, PVC) + trade size
calculation_procedure.output: Total internal area (in²) at 100%; allowable area at 31%/40%/53% pre-computed in Table 4

calculation_procedure.rule: Total conductor area ≤ raceway allowable area at the appropriate % fill

worked_example.scenario: Run 9 conductors of #6 AWG THHN through an EMT raceway. Determine minimum trade size.
worked_example._note: If 1 inch EMT is preferred for space reasons, reduce to 8 conductors max (8 × 0.0507 = 0.406 in² > 0.346 → still over). 1-inch EMT max with #6 THHN is 6 conductors (6 × 0.0507 = 0.304 < 0.346 ✓)

special_cases.procedure: Sum each size's area separately, then sum all
Same — each conductor's area from Table 5 column for its type

special_cases.case: Conductors with internal grounding conductor (NM cable, MC cable)
special_cases.procedure: Use the cable's total external diameter, not individual conductor sums (cables are treated as a unit)

special_cases.case: Communications cables in same conduit as power
special_cases.procedure: Generally prohibited per 800.133 (≥2 in separation required); when permitted (e.g. fiber), apply fill calc to ALL cables in the raceway

common_errors: Using copper kcmil area instead of external area (with insulation) — frequent error
Using 53% (1-conductor) fill % for a single piece of wire in a raceway with planned future expansion — once future conductors added, 40% rule applies
Forgetting to include the EGC as a counted conductor for fill — EGC contributes to total area
Mixing fill % for different conductors — the entire raceway is single-fill-percent based on the highest conductor count
iec_60364_cross_reference: IEC 60364-5-52 conduit/trunking fill handled by Annex B in some national supplements (e.g. BS 7671 Appendix 4 + Appendix 6 trunking fill); the IEC base does not have a comprehensive fill table comparable to NEC Chapter 9 Table 1 + 4 + 5. NEC's approach is more codified.
